title: galium aparine subsp. infestum
intro: Galium aparine subsp. infestum, also known as stickyweed or cleavers, is a plant species that belongs to the family Rubiaceae and is native to Europe and Asia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Galium aparine subsp. infestum include reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
